session_hint: the full-text search engine decision for the product catalog
question: What search engine did we end up choosing for the product catalog?
turns: Product catalog needs full-text search with faceted filtering on category, price range, and brand. Elasticsearch is the industry standard — planning to self-host a 3-node cluster on EC2.
ES cluster provisioned, index mapping defined with keyword fields for facets and text fields for full-text. Kibana hooked up for monitoring. Initial results look good.
After two weeks the cluster requires constant tuning — JVM heap sizing, shard count adjustments, GC pause alerts. DevOps time is being eaten alive and we have 500k products, not petabytes.

We're scrapping Elasticsearch. Going with Typesense instead — single binary, instant setup, search-as-you-type out of the box, and our entire catalog fits comfortably in RAM. DevOps overhead drops to near zero. Final decision: Typesense.
